feedback_files.tests/test_feedback_grant_boundaries.py:
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).parents[1]


def sql(name):
    return (ROOT / "sql" / name).read_text(encoding="utf-8")


def database():
    connection = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
    connection.executescript(sql("schema.sql"))
    return connection


def grant(tenant, start=10, end=20):
    return {
        "tenant_id": tenant,
        "principal_id": "shared-user",
        "capability": "approve",
        "valid_from": start,
        "valid_until": end,
        "revoked_at": None,
    }


def test_schema_identity_includes_tenant():
    db = database()
    statement = """
        INSERT INTO access_grants
        (tenant_id, principal_id, capability, valid_from, valid_until)
        VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
    """
    db.execute(statement, ("red", "duplicate", "read", 0, 10))
    db.execute(statement, ("blue", "duplicate", "read", 0, 10))
    count = db.execute("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM access_grants").fetchone()[0]
    assert count == 2


def test_upsert_keeps_same_identity_in_both_tenants():
    db = database()
    db.execute(sql("upsert_grant.sql"), grant("red"))
    db.execute(sql("upsert_grant.sql"), grant("blue", start=30, end=40))
    tenants = {
        row[0] for row in db.execute("SELECT tenant_id FROM access_grants")
    }
    assert tenants == {"red", "blue"}


def test_expiration_boundary_is_not_effective():
    db = database()
    db.execute(sql("upsert_grant.sql"), grant("edge", start=80, end=90))
    rows = db.execute(
        sql("effective_access.sql"),
        {"tenant_id": "edge", "principal_id": "shared-user", "as_of": 90},
    ).fetchall()
    assert rows == []
